rulings.Case Title: AMBER YUST VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, A et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 3, 2011, line 9, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ACTING BY AND THROUGH, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action is overruled. The Court finds that Defendant DVM is operationally similar to a School District (both interact directly with the public and provides an affirmative service to the public) and dissimilar to a prison. Accordingly the Court relies on Nicole M. By and Through Jacqueline M. v. Martinez Unified School District., N.D. Cal. 1997, 964 F.Supp.1369 which held that a school district is a ¿business establishment¿ for purposes of the Unruh Civil Rights Act. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support the argument that the DMV is a business establishment to survive a Demurrer. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that the harm she suffered was foreseeable with the scope of DeMartini¿s employment with the DMV. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd and 4th causes of action is overruled. These are properly plead and present questions of fact that are not appropriate for determination at this stage of the proceedings. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. =(302/LMG)
